description: next/ v2.0 — Level-3 MOM Workorder (ISA-95 Part 4 / B2MML WorkRequest). TERMINOLOGY RULE : when a FabricationOrder runs across multiple time slots, each slot produces an 'index' (index 1, 2, 3 ...) — and 'each index equals one Workorder in ISA-95; it is a subdivision of the FabricationOrder'. This profile models exactly that subdivision: a Workorder is a time-bounded sub-piece of a ProductionOrder (OperationsRequest). It binds the MaterialLots produced/consumed per slot. (Note: 'batch' is NOT an ISA-95 term — the physical material piece is a MaterialLot, the activity is this Workorder.)

attributes.name: workorder_no
attributes.description: Identity of the Workorder (join key). Recommendation: <production_order_no>.<order_index>.

attributes.name: production_order_ref
attributes.description: FK to ProductionOrder.production_order_no (PART_OF). The FabricationOrder that this sub-lot subdivides.

attributes.name: order_index
attributes.description: The FabricationOrder index (the MES FOIndex) = the slot number of this Workorder.

relationships.description: The ProductionOrder (OperationsRequest) that this Workorder subdivides.
Inverse of MaterialLot.PRODUCED_BY — the material lots created in this slot.

isa95.note: ISA-95 Part 4 WorkRequest = dispatch subdivision of an OperationsRequest. RULE: FabricationOrder index == Workorder (subdivision of the FO across time slots). Creates/consumes MaterialLots. Relationship PART_OF to the ProductionOrder = ISA-95 SUBDIVISION_OF (order sub-lot / FO index).
